science.title: NASA's Discovery in the Sahara Reveals Hidden Geological Wonder
science.description: NASA's Earth Observatory has revealed that the concentric rocky rings of Mount Arkanu in the Sahara were formed by ancient magma intrusions, challenging previous theories of an extraterrestrial origin.

science.title: Racetrack-shaped lasers developed for bright, stable frequency combs
science.description: A new, miniature laser source developed by applied physicists in the Harvard John A. Paulson School of Engineering and Applied Sciences (SEAS) and the Technical University of Vienna (TU Wien) could soon pack the power of a laboratory-based spectrometer—an important workhorse tool for precision environmental gas analysis—onto a single microchip.

science.title: Sea slug study reveals the best way for humans to boost memory

science.title: Manitoba Anishinaabe artist designs patch for Canadian astronaut ahead of Artemis II launch

science.title: Human touch leaves chicks feeling happy, study finds
science.description: Chicks, just like pets, also benefit from gentle human touch, new research in the UK has revealed.  Scientists at the University of Bristol have discovered that gentle human interactions do not only prevent fear in baby chicks but also trigger positive emotions. ai.title: I think Google is taking a couple digs at OpenAI about Sora.
ai.description:
OpenAI gave up on Sora last week, but in a post about Google’s new “cost efficient” Veo 3.1 Lite AI video model, DeepMind staffer Logan Kilpatrick says that “video’s here to stay.” And in a blog post about the model, the authors discuss Google’s “commitment to making video generation more available to developers.”
[Link: Build with Veo 3.1 Lite, our most cost-effective video generation model | https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/ai/veo-3-1-lite/ | Google]
